meta.name: No-Knead Dutch Oven Bread
meta.subtitle: Minimal effort • great first artisan loaf • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: Canonical baseline for an easy no-knead loaf baked in a Dutch oven. Long fermentation builds flavor with minimal effort.

tasteProfile.notes: Simple wheaty flavor with mild tang from long fermentation; crisp crackly crust from Dutch oven steam.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (or bread flour; or fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: AP gives a tender crumb; bread flour gives more chew and slightly better rise.

ingredients.label: Water (room temperature)
ingredients.notes: About 80% hydration. Hold back 20–30g for fresh milled flour; add only if dough feels too stiff.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (or active dry)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 teaspoon. Long fermentation does the heavy lifting. If using active dry, you can mix it in dry (no need to bloom for this recipe).

ingredients.label: Flour for dusting (AP or rice flour)
Parchment paper (for transfer, optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional but highly recommended for safe transfer into a hot Dutch oven.

steps.title: Mix (no kneading)
instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, salt, and yeast.
Add water and stir until no dry flour remains. The dough will look shaggy and sticky.
Cover the bowl.

steps.title: Long rise (bulk fermentation)
instructions: Let dough rise at room temperature 12–18 hours.
It’s ready when the surface is bubbly, the dough has expanded significantly, and it looks loose and airy.
Cool rooms may need closer to 18 hours; warm rooms may be ready closer to 12.

steps.title: Shape (gentle)
instructions: Dust a work surface with flour. Scrape dough out of the bowl.
With floured hands, fold the dough over itself a few times to form a rough ball. Keep it gentle—don’t punch down aggressively.
Option A (simple): place seam-side down on a lightly floured surface for the bench rest.
Option B (clean transfer): place on parchment and shape into a round.

steps.title: Bench rest / short proof
instructions: Cover loosely and rest 45–90 minutes until slightly puffy.
Meanwhile, preheat the Dutch oven in the oven.

steps.title: Preheat Dutch oven
instructions: Place Dutch oven (with lid) in the oven and preheat to 450°F (232°C) for 30–45 minutes.
A thoroughly preheated pot is key to oven spring and crust.

steps.title: Bake (covered then uncovered)
instructions: Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven.
Transfer dough into the pot: easiest is lifting by parchment; otherwise, flip dough seam-side down quickly into the pot.
Cover with lid and bake 30 minutes.
Remove lid and bake 12–18 minutes more until deeply golden brown.
Optional doneness check: internal temperature ~205–210°F (96–99°C).

steps.title: Cool completely
instructions: Remove bread to a rack and cool at least 90 minutes before slicing.
Cutting too early can make the crumb gummy.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour absorbs more water and can ferment faster; bran can reduce rise unless you adjust technique.
guidelines: For best beginner results, use a blend: 70–85% fresh milled flour + 15–30% bread/AP flour.
Hold back 20–30g water; add only if dough is stiff after mixing. Fresh milled can look dry at first then loosen as it hydrates.
If using 100% fresh milled, expect a slightly tighter crumb and less loft; consider extending the bench rest and using a tighter shaping fold.
If your room is warm, shorten the bulk fermentation (check at 10–12 hours) to avoid over-fermentation.

storage.counter: Best day-of for crust. Store cut-side down on a board or loosely wrapped 1 day; then bag to prevent drying.
storage.freezer: Slice and freeze up to 2–3 months. Toast from frozen.
storage.refresh: To re-crisp crust: spritz lightly with water and bake at 400°F (205°C) for 8–12 minutes.

variations.name: More flavor (tiny whole wheat)
variations.change: Use 400g AP/bread flour + 30g whole wheat flour; keep water the same (or add 5–10g if dough feels stiff).
variations.notes: Slightly more rustic flavor without changing the method.

variations.name: Faster schedule (same-day-ish)
variations.change: Increase yeast to 3g and ferment 4–6 hours (until bubbly and expanded), then proceed as written.
variations.notes: Less tang and slightly less complex flavor than the long-rise version.

variations.name: Seeded loaf
variations.change: Add 30–60g mixed seeds (sesame, flax, sunflower) during initial mix.
variations.notes: If adding many seeds, you may need +10–20g water.

notes: The dough is intentionally wet and sticky—this is normal for no-knead bread and helps create holes in the crumb.
If the loaf spreads too much, your dough may be over-fermented or too wet; shorten bulk next time or reduce water by 15–25g.
If the loaf is dense, your yeast may be weak or the dough under-fermented; extend bulk or ensure yeast is fresh.

audit.testNotes: Canonical no-knead loaf using long fermentation for flavor and Dutch oven steam for crust. Designed for first-time artisan success.
